languages.name: Common
languages.notes: Lingua franca of educated medieval Europe. The universal language of the church, scholarship, and international trade. Not everyone speaks it — peasants and rural folk often speak only their regional tongue.

languages.name: Latin
languages.notes: Ecclesiastical Latin — slightly different register from Common but mutually intelligible with educated speakers. Language of the Catholic church, formal documents, and scholarly works.
languages.knownBy: Brother Aldric, educated clergy, university scholars

languages.name: Slovak
languages.notes: Regional language of the Carpathian area. In 1274 nearly identical to Old Czech — speakers of one understand the other without difficulty. Partially intelligible with Polish and Old Russian.
languages.knownBy: Old Gregor, Marta, Tomas, Piotr, local Carpathian NPCs

languages.name: German
languages.notes: Dominant trade and nobility language of Central Europe. The language of the Holy Roman Empire, Habsburg court, and most merchant guilds. Katya knows it as a professional necessity.
languages.knownBy: Katya Voss, Henryk the Merchant, Habsburg nobility, guild merchants

languages.name: English
languages.notes: Middle English — uncommon in Central Europe. Brother Aldric's native tongue. Zarina's father was English — she grew up hearing his idealized stories of England though not the language itself.

languages.name: French
languages.notes: Court and noble language of Western Europe. Language of the troubadour tradition. Katya knows songs in Old French — she performs them even if her audience cannot understand the words. The sound is what matters.
languages.knownBy: Katya Voss (songs and performance only), Western European nobility, Vladislav

languages.name: Magyar
languages.notes: Administrative language of the Kingdom of Hungary. Completely unrelated to any Slavic or Germanic language — a linguistic island in Central Europe. Required to deal with Hungarian nobility, castle staff, and border officials. Will become critical at Orava Castle.
languages.knownBy: Hungarian nobles, Orava Castle staff, border officials, King Ladislaus IV's court

languages.name: Old Russian
languages.notes: Language of Kievan Rus traders and refugees. The Mongol invasion 25 years ago shattered Kievan Rus — survivors and refugees spread across Central Europe. Partially intelligible with Slovak and Polish. A Rus speaker and a Slovak speaker can manage basic communication with effort.
languages.knownBy: Rus traders, Orthodox clergy, Mongol-displaced refugees, Vladislav

languages.name: Polish
languages.notes: Northern Slavic language. Partially intelligible with Slovak and Czech. Polish merchants and pilgrims travel through this region regularly.

languages.name: Church Slavonic
languages.notes: Liturgical language of the Eastern Orthodox church. Created in 863 AD by Saints Cyril and Methodius. Partially intelligible with modern Slavic languages. Vladislav knew its creators personally — this is a tell for anyone paying attention to his age.
languages.knownBy: Vladislav Dragan, Orthodox clergy, educated Eastern church scholars

languages.name: Cuman
languages.notes: Steppe language of the Cuman people — Turkic family, related to other steppe tongues. King Ladislaus IV of Hungary was raised speaking Cuman and is more comfortable in it than Magyar. Rusza the horse trader speaks it natively. Hearing it in a Carpathian inn in 1274 means something about where a person came from and why they're here.
languages.knownBy: Rusza, Cuman refugees, King Ladislaus IV's court, some Hungarian border soldiers

languages.name: Yiddish
languages.notes: Language of Jewish communities throughout Central Europe. Germanic base with Hebrew and Slavic elements. Jewish merchants are present throughout this trade region — knowing Yiddish opens certain doors.
languages.knownBy: Jewish merchant communities, some German speakers

languages.name: Greek
languages.notes: Language of the Byzantine Empire and the Eastern church. Academic, theological, and diplomatic use. The Byzantine Empire is declining but Greek remains the language of Eastern scholarship. Vladislav has 600 years of reason to speak it.
languages.knownBy: Vladislav, educated clergy, Byzantine traders, Eastern church scholars

languages.name: Arabic
languages.notes: Via trade routes, Crusade contact, and mathematical and philosophical scholarship. Arabic numerals and algebra reached Europe through Arabic texts. Rare in Central Europe but not unknown among traveling scholars.
languages.knownBy: Vladislav, traveling scholars, spice merchants from the east

languages.name: Elvish (Americas)
languages.notes: The language of Zarina's mother's people. Completely unknown to all Europeans — not a variant of any known Elvish tongue, a fully separate language family from a continent Europeans do not know exists. To European ears it sounds like no language they have ever encountered. Zarina grew up speaking it alongside English stories from her father.
languages.specialRule: No European NPC recognizes this as a language they know. Vladislav has heard something like it before — once, long ago. He does not say where or when.

languages.name: Draconic
languages.notes: The universal dragon tongue. Valid everywhere in the world — it predates human civilization on every continent. Spurt speaks it natively as a Kobold Sorcerer. No NPC in Session 0 speaks it, but some recognize it. Vladislav recognizes it immediately and his expression shifts.
languages.specialRule: Vladislav immediately recognizes Draconic if spoken. His expression shifts. He says nothing but he is now more interested in Spurt than before. Flag as HIGH priority Max whisper.

languages.name: Giant
languages.notes: The language of giants and their kin — also spoken by firbolgs (Marfire's people). No European speakers in 1274 Central Europe.

languages.name: Speech of Beast and Leaf
languages.notes: Firbolg racial — limited communication with beasts and plants. Not a spoken language other humanoids can learn.

languages.name: Infernal
languages.notes: The language of devils. Tieflings know it innately. In this setting no living NPC speaks it — if it's heard, something is very wrong.

languages.name: Abyssal
languages.notes: The language of demons and the Abyss. Similar concern as Infernal — if heard in 1274 Europe, a Bad Thing is in the room.

languages.name: Celestial
languages.notes: Language of good-aligned outsiders — angels, devas. A blessed tongue in Church theology.

languages.name: Czech
languages.notes: West Slavic language, nearly identical to Old Slovak in 1274. Mutually intelligible with Slovak, partially with Polish.

languages.name: Aglarondan
languages.notes: A Forgotten Realms language carried over from Chazz's character sheet. No speakers in 1274 Europe — capability marker only.

languages.name: Chondathan
languages.notes: A Forgotten Realms language from Chazz's character sheet. No speakers in 1274 Europe — capability marker only.
